996→type FunctionResponse struct {
   997→	// Optional. Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned,
   998→	// turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function
   999→	// calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false,
  1000→	// the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON_BLOCKING
  1001→	// function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not
  1002→	// be considered. It is allowed to return empty `response` with `will_continue=False`
  1003→	// to signal that the function call is finished.
  1004→	WillContinue *bool `json:"willContinue,omitempty"`
  1005→	// Optional. Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation. Only
  1006→	// applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. Defaults to WHEN_IDLE.
  1007→	Scheduling FunctionResponseScheduling `json:"scheduling,omitempty"`
  1008→	// Optional. List of parts that constitute a function response. Each part may
  1009→	// have a different IANA MIME type.
  1010→	Parts []*FunctionResponsePart `json:"parts,omitempty"`
  1011→	// Optional. The ID of the function call this response is for. Populated by the client
  1012→	// to match the corresponding function call `id`.
  1013→	ID string `json:"id,omitempty"`
  1014→	// Required. The name of the function to call. Matches [FunctionDeclaration.name] and
  1015→	// [FunctionCall.name].
  1016→	Name string `json:"name,omitempty"`
  1017→	// Required. The function response in JSON object format. Use "output" key to specify
  1018→	// function output and "error" key to specify error details (if any). If "output" and
  1019→	// "error" keys are not specified, then whole "response" is treated as function output.
  1020→	Response map[string]any `json:"response,omitempty"`
  1021→}
  1022→
  1023→// Content blob.
  1024→type Blob struct {
  1025→	// Required. Raw bytes.
  1026→	Data []byte `json:"data,omitempty"`
  1027→	// Optional. Display name of the blob. Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish
  1028→	// blobs. This field is only returned in PromptMessage for prompt management. It is
  1029→	// currently used in the Gemini GenerateContent calls only when server side tools (code_execution,
  1030→	// google_search, and url_context) are enabled. This field is not supported in Gemini
  1031→	// API.
  1032→	DisplayName string `json:"displayName,omitempty"`
  1033→	// Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.
  1034→	MIMEType string `json:"mimeType,omitempty"`
  1035→}
  1036→
  1037→// Metadata describes the input video content.
  1038→type VideoMetadata struct {
  1039→	// Optional. The end offset of the video.
  1040→	EndOffset time.Duration `json:"endOffset,omitempty"`
  1041→	// Optional. The frame rate of the video sent to the model. If not specified, the default
  1042→	// value will be 1.0. The FPS range is (0.0, 24.0].
  1043→	FPS *float64 `json:"fps,omitempty"`
  1044→	// Optional. The start offset of the video.
  1045→	StartOffset time.Duration `json:"startOffset,omitempty"`

917→type FunctionCall struct {
   918→	// Optional. The unique ID of the function call. If populated, the client to execute
   919→	// the
   920→	// `function_call` and return the response with the matching `id`.
   921→	ID string `json:"id,omitempty"`
   922→	// Optional. The function parameters and values in JSON object format. See [FunctionDeclaration.parameters]
   923→	// for parameter details.
   924→	Args map[string]any `json:"args,omitempty"`
   925→	// Optional. Required. The name of the function to call. Matches [FunctionDeclaration.Name].
   926→	Name string `json:"name,omitempty"`
   927→	// Optional. The partial argument value of the function call. If provided, represents
   928→	// the arguments/fields that are streamed incrementally. This field is not supported
   929→	// in Gemini API.
   930→	PartialArgs []*PartialArg `json:"partialArgs,omitempty"`
   931→	// Optional. Whether this is the last part of the FunctionCall. If true, another partial
   932→	// message for the current FunctionCall is expected to follow. This field is not supported
   933→	// in Gemini API.
   934→	WillContinue *bool `json:"willContinue,omitempty"`
   935→}
   936→
   937→// Raw media bytes for function response.
   938→// Text should not be sent as raw bytes, use the FunctionResponse.response
   939→// field.
   940→type FunctionResponseBlob struct {
   941→	// Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.
   942→	MIMEType string `json:"mimeType,omitempty"`
   943→	// Required. Inline media bytes.
   944→	Data []byte `json:"data,omitempty"`
   945→	// Optional. Display name of the blob.
   946→	// Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish blobs.
   947→	DisplayName string `json:"displayName,omitempty"`
   948→}
   949→
   950→// URI based data for function response.
   951→type FunctionResponseFileData struct {
   952→	// Required. URI.
   953→	FileURI string `json:"fileUri,omitempty"`
   954→	// Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.
   955→	MIMEType string `json:"mimeType,omitempty"`
   956→	// Optional. Display name of the file.
   957→	// Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish files.
   958→	DisplayName string `json:"displayName,omitempty"`
   959→}
   960→
   961→// A datatype containing media that is part of a `FunctionResponse` message.
   962→// A `FunctionResponsePart` consists of data which has an associated datatype. A
   963→// `FunctionResponsePart` can only contain one of the accepted types in
   964→// `FunctionResponsePart.data`.
   965→// A `FunctionResponsePart` must have a fixed IANA MIME type identifying the
   966→// type and subtype of the media if the `inline_data` field is filled with raw
   967→// bytes.
   968→type FunctionResponsePart struct {
   969→	// Optional. Inline media bytes.
   970→	InlineData *FunctionResponseBlob `json:"inlineData,omitempty"`
   971→	// Optional. URI based data.
   972→	FileData *FunctionResponseFileData `json:"fileData,omitempty"`
   973→}
   974→
   975→// NewFunctionResponsePartFromURI builds a FunctionResponsePart from a given file URI and mime type.
   976→func NewFunctionResponsePartFromURI(fileURI, mimeType string) *FunctionResponsePart {
   977→	return &FunctionResponsePart{
   978→		FileData: &FunctionResponseFileData{
   979→			FileURI:  fileURI,
   980→			MIMEType: mimeType,
   981→		},
   982→	}
   983→}
   984→
   985→// NewFunctionResponsePartFromBytes builds a FunctionResponsePart from a given byte array and mime type.
   986→func NewFunctionResponsePartFromBytes(data []byte, mimeType string) *FunctionResponsePart {
   987→	return &FunctionResponsePart{
   988→		InlineData: &FunctionResponseBlob{
   989→			Data:     data,
   990→			MIMEType: mimeType,
   991→		},
   992→	}
   993→}
   994→

content.content: 1883→type Tool struct { 1884→ // Optional. List of function declarations that the tool supports. 1885→ FunctionDeclarations []*FunctionDeclaration `json:"functionDeclarations,omitempty"` 1886→ // Optional. Retrieval tool type. System will always execute the provided retrieval 1887→ // tool(s) to get external knowledge to answer the prompt. Retrieval results are presented 1888→ // to the model for generation. This field is not supported in Gemini API. 1889→ Retrieval *Retrieval `json:"retrieval,omitempty"` 1890→ // Optional. Specialized retrieval tool that is powered by Google Search. 1891→ GoogleSearchRetrieval *GoogleSearchRetrieval `json:"googleSearchRetrieval,omitempty"` 1892→ // Optional. Tool to support the model interacting directly with the 1893→ // computer. If enabled, it automatically populates computer-use specific 1894→ // Function Declarations. 1895→ ComputerUse *ComputerUse `json:"computerUse,omitempty"` 1896→ // Optional. Tool to retrieve knowledge from the File Search Stores. 1897→ FileSearch *FileSearch `json:"fileSearch,omitempty"` 1898→ // Optional. CodeExecution tool type. Enables the model to execute code as part of generation. 1899→ CodeExecution *ToolCodeExecution `json:"codeExecution,omitempty"` 1900→ // Optional. Tool to support searching public web data, powered by Vertex AI Search 1901→ // and Sec4 compliance. This field is not supported in Gemini API. 1902→ EnterpriseWebSearch *EnterpriseWebSearch `json:"enterpriseWebSearch,omitempty"` 1903→ // Optional. GoogleMaps tool type. Tool to support Google Maps in Model. 1904→ GoogleMaps *GoogleMaps `json:"googleMaps,omitempty"` 1905→ // Optional. GoogleSearch tool type. Tool to support Google Search in Model. Powered 1906→ // by Google. 1907→ GoogleSearch *GoogleSearch `json:"googleSearch,omitempty"` 1908→ // Optional. Tool to support URL context retrieval. 1909→ URLContext *URLContext `json:"urlContext,omitempty"` 1910→} 1911→ 1912→// Function calling config. 1913→type FunctionCallingConfig struct { 1914→ // Optional. Function calling mode. 1915→ Mode FunctionCallingConfigMode `json:"mode,omitempty"` 1916→ // Optional. Function names to call. Only set when the Mode is ANY. Function names should 1917→ // match [FunctionDeclaration.Name]. With mode set to ANY, model will predict a function 1918→ // call from the set of function names provided. 1919→ AllowedFunctionNames []string `json:"allowedFunctionNames,omitempty"` 1920→ // Optional. When set to true, arguments of a single function call will be streamed 1921→ // out in multiple parts/contents/responses. Partial parameter results will be returned 1922→ // in the [FunctionCall.partial_args] field. This field is not supported in Gemini API. 1923→ StreamFunctionCallArguments *bool `json:"streamFunctionCallArguments,omitempty"` 1924→} 1925→ 1926→// An object that represents a latitude/longitude pair. 1927→// This is expressed as a pair of doubles to represent degrees latitude and 1928→// degrees longitude. Unless specified otherwise, this object must conform to the 1929→// 1930→// WGS84 standard. Values must be within normalized ranges. 1931→type LatLng struct { 1932→ // Optional. The latitude in degrees. It must be in the range [-90.0, +90.0]. 1933→ Latitude *float64 `json:"latitude,omitempty"` 1934→ // Optional. The longitude in degrees. It must be in the range [-180.0, +180.0] 1935→ Longitude *float64 `json:"longitude,omitempty"` 1936→} 1937→ 1938→// Retrieval config. 1939→type RetrievalConfig struct { 1940→ // Optional. The location of the user. 1941→ LatLng *LatLng `json:"latLng,omitempty"` 1942→ // The language code of the user. 1943→ LanguageCode string `json:"languageCode,omitempty"` 1944→} 1945→ 1946→// Tool config. 1947→// This config is shared for all tools provided in the request. 1948→type ToolConfig struct { 1949→ // Optional. Function calling config. 1950→ FunctionCallingConfig *FunctionCallingConfig `json:"functionCallingConfig,omitempty"` 1951→ // Optional. Retrieval config. 1952→ RetrievalConfig *RetrievalConfig `json:"retrievalConfig,omitempty"` 1953→} 1954→ 1955→// ReplicatedVoiceConfig is used to configure replicated voice. 1956→type ReplicatedVoiceConfig struct { 1957→ // Optional. The MIME type of the replicated voice. 1958→ MIMEType string `json:"mimeType,omitempty"` 1959→ // Optional. The sample audio of the replicated voice. 1960→ VoiceSampleAudio []byte `json:"voiceSampleAudio,omitempty"` 1961→} 1962→ 1963→// The configuration for the prebuilt speaker to use. 1964→type PrebuiltVoiceConfig struct { 1965→ // The name of the preset voice to use. 1966→ VoiceName string `json:"voiceName,omitempty"` 1967→} 1968→ 1969→type VoiceConfig struct { 1970→ // Optional. If true, the model will use a replicated voice for the response. 1971→ ReplicatedVoiceConfig *ReplicatedVoiceConfig `json:"replicatedVoiceConfig,omitempty"` 1972→ // The configuration for the prebuilt voice to use. 1973→ PrebuiltVoiceConfig *PrebuiltVoiceConfig `json:"prebuiltVoiceConfig,omitempty"` 1974→} 1975→ 1976→// Configuration for a single speaker in a multi speaker setup. 1977→type SpeakerVoiceConfig struct { 1978→ // Required. The name of the speaker. This should be the same as the speaker name used 1979→ // in the prompt. 1980→ Speaker string `json:"speaker,omitempty"` 1981→ // Required. The configuration for the voice of this speaker. 1982→ VoiceConfig *VoiceConfig `json:"voiceConfig,omitempty"` 1983→} 1984→ 1985→// Configuration for a multi-speaker text-to-speech request. 1986→type MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig struct { 1987→ // Required. A list of configurations for the voices of the speakers. Exactly two speaker 1988→ // voice configurations must be provided. 1989→ SpeakerVoiceConfigs []*SpeakerVoiceConfig `json:"speakerVoiceConfigs,omitempty"` 1990→} 1991→ 1992→type SpeechConfig struct { 1993→ // Optional. Configuration for the voice of the response. 1994→ VoiceConfig *VoiceConfig `json:"voiceConfig,omitempty"` 1995→ // Optional. Language code (ISO 639. e.g. en-US) for the speech synthesization. 1996→ LanguageCode string `json:"languageCode,omitempty"` 1997→ // The configuration for a multi-speaker text-to-speech request. This field is mutually 1998→ // exclusive with `voice_config`. 1999→ MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig *MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig `json:"multiSpeakerVoiceConfig,omitempty"` 2000→} 2001→ 2002→// The thinking features configuration. 2003→type ThinkingConfig struct { 2004→ // Optional. Indicates whether to include thoughts in the response. If true, thoughts 2005→ // are returned only if the model supports thought and thoughts are available. 2006→ IncludeThoughts bool `json:"includeThoughts,omitempty"` 2007→ // Optional. Indicates the thinking budget in tokens. 2008→ ThinkingBudget *int32 `json:"thinkingBudget,omitempty"` 2009→ // Optional. The number of thoughts tokens that the model should generate. 2010→ ThinkingLevel ThinkingLevel `json:"thinkingLevel,omitempty"` 2011→} 2012→ 2013→// The image generation configuration to be used in GenerateContentConfig. 2014→type ImageConfig struct { 2015→ // Optional. Aspect ratio of the generated images. Supported values are 2016→ // "1:1", "2:3", "3:2", "3:4", "4:3", "9:16", "16:9", and "21:9". 2017→ AspectRatio string `json:"aspectRatio,omitempty"` 2018→ // Optional. Specifies the size of generated images. Supported 2019→ // values are `1K`, `2K`, `4K`. If not specified, the model will use default 2020→ // value `1K`. 2021→ ImageSize string `json:"imageSize,omitempty"` 2022→ // Optional. MIME type of the generated image. This field is not 2023→ // supported in Gemini API. 2024→ OutputMIMEType string `json:"outputMimeType,omitempty"` 2025→ // Optional. Compression quality of the generated image (for 2026→ // ``image/jpeg`` only). This field is not supported in Gemini API. 2027→ OutputCompressionQuality *int32 `json:"outputCompressionQuality,omitempty"` 2028→} 2029→ 2030→// When automated routing is specified, the routing will be determined by the pretrained 2031→// routing model and customer provided model routing preference. This data type is not 2032→// supported in Gemini API. 2033→type GenerationConfigRoutingConfigAutoRoutingMode struct { 2034→ // The model routing preference. 2035→ ModelRoutingPreference string `json:"modelRoutingPreference,omitempty"` 2036→} 2037→ 2038→// When manual routing is set, the specified model will be used directly. This data 2039→// type is not supported in Gemini API. 2040→type GenerationConfigRoutingConfigManualRoutingMode struct { 2041→ // The model name to use. Only the public LLM models are accepted. See [Supported models](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/model-reference/inference#supported-models). 2042→ ModelName string `json:"modelName,omitempty"` 2043→} 2044→ 2045→// The configuration for routing the request to a specific model. This data type is 2046→// not supported in Gemini API. 2047→type GenerationConfigRoutingConfig struct { 2048→ // Automated routing. 2049→ AutoMode *GenerationConfigRoutingConfigAutoRoutingMode `json:"autoMode,omitempty"` 2050→ // Manual routing. 2051→ ManualMode *GenerationConfigRoutingConfigManualRoutingMode `json:"manualMode,omitempty"` 2052→} 2053→ 2054→// Safety settings. 2055→type SafetySetting struct { 2056→ // Required. Harm category. 2057→ Category HarmCategory `json:"category,omitempty"` 2058→ // Optional. Specify if the threshold is used for probability or severity score. If 2059→ // not specified, the threshold is used for probability score. This field is not supported 2060→ // in Gemini API. 2061→ Method HarmBlockMethod `json:"method,omitempty"` 2062→ // Required. content.content:
2069→type GenerateContentConfig struct {
  2070→	// Optional. Used to override HTTP request options.
  2071→	HTTPOptions *HTTPOptions `json:"httpOptions,omitempty"`
  2072→	// Optional. Instructions for the model to steer it toward better performance.
  2073→	// For example, "Answer as concisely as possible" or "Don't use technical
  2074→	// terms in your response".
  2075→	SystemInstruction *Content `json:"systemInstruction,omitempty"`
  2076→	// Optional. Value that controls the degree of randomness in token selection.
  2077→	// Lower temperatures are good for prompts that require a less open-ended or
  2078→	// creative response, while higher temperatures can lead to more diverse or
  2079→	// creative results.
  2080→	Temperature *float32 `json:"temperature,omitempty"`
  2081→	// Optional. Tokens are selected from the most to least probable until the sum
  2082→	// of their probabilities equals this value. Use a lower value for less
  2083→	// random responses and a higher value for more random responses.
  2084→	TopP *float32 `json:"topP,omitempty"`
  2085→	// Optional. For each token selection step, the ``top_k`` tokens with the
  2086→	// highest probabilities are sampled. Then tokens are further filtered based
  2087→	// on ``top_p`` with the final token selected using temperature sampling. Use
  2088→	// a lower number for less random responses and a higher number for more
  2089→	// random responses.
  2090→	TopK *float32 `json:"topK,omitempty"`
  2091→	// Optional. Number of response variations to return.
  2092→	// If empty, the system will choose a default value (currently 1).
  2093→	CandidateCount int32 `json:"candidateCount,omitempty"`
  2094→	// Optional. Maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the response.
  2095→	// If empty, API will use a default value. The default value varies by model.
  2096→	MaxOutputTokens int32 `json:"maxOutputTokens,omitempty"`
  2097→	// Optional. List of strings that tells the model to stop generating text if one
  2098→	// of the strings is encountered in the response.
  2099→	StopSequences []string `json:"stopSequences,omitempty"`
  2100→	// Optional. Whether to return the log probabilities of the tokens that were
  2101→	// chosen by the model at each step.
  2102→	ResponseLogprobs bool `json:"responseLogprobs,omitempty"`
  2103→	// Optional. Number of top candidate tokens to return the log probabilities for
  2104→	// at each generation step.
  2105→	Logprobs *int32 `json:"logprobs,omitempty"`
  2106→	// Optional. Positive values penalize tokens that already appear in the
  2107→	// generated text, increasing the probability of generating more diverse
  2108→	// content.
  2109→	PresencePenalty *float32 `json:"presencePenalty,omitempty"`
  2110→	// Optional. Positive values penalize tokens that repeatedly appear in the
  2111→	// generated text, increasing the probability of generating more diverse
  2112→	// content.
  2113→	FrequencyPenalty *float32 `json:"frequencyPenalty,omitempty"`
  2114→	// Optional. When ``seed`` is fixed to a specific number, the model makes a best
  2115→	// effort to provide the same response for repeated requests. By default, a
  2116→	// random number is used.
  2117→	Seed *int32 `json:"seed,omitempty"`
  2118→	// Optional. Output response mimetype of the generated candidate text.
  2119→	// Supported mimetype:
  2120→	//   - `text/plain`: (default) Text output.
  2121→	//   - `application/json`: JSON response in the candidates.
  2122→	// The model needs to be prompted to output the appropriate response type,
  2123→	// otherwise the behavior is undefined.
  2124→	// This is a preview feature.
  2125→	ResponseMIMEType string `json:"responseMimeType,omitempty"`
  2126→	// Optional. The `Schema` object allows the definition of input and output data types.
  2127→	// These types can be objects, but also primitives and arrays.
  2128→	// Represents a select subset of an [OpenAPI 3.0 schema
  2129→	// object](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#schema).
  2130→	// If set, a compatible response_mime_type must also be set.
  2131→	// Compatible mimetypes: `application/json`: Schema for JSON response.
  2132→	// If `response_schema` doesn't process your schema correctly, try using
  2133→	// `response_json_schema` instead.
  2134→	ResponseSchema *Schema `json:"responseSchema,omitempty"`
  2135→	// Optional. Output schema of the generated response.
  2136→	// This is an alternative to `response_schema` that accepts [JSON
  2137→	// Schema](https://json-schema.org/). If set, `response_schema` must be
  2138→	// omitted, but `response_mime_type` is required. While the full JSON Schema
  2139→	// may be sent, not all features are supported. Specifically, only the
  2140→	// following properties are supported: - `$id` - `$defs` - `$ref` - `$anchor`
  2141→	//   - `type` - `format` - `title` - `description` - `enum` (for strings and
  2142→	// numbers) - `items` - `prefixItems` - `minItems` - `maxItems` - `minimum` -
  2143→	// `maximum` - `anyOf` - `oneOf` (interpreted the same as `anyOf`) -
  2144→	// `properties` - `additionalProperties` - `required` The non-standard
  2145→	// `propertyOrdering` property may also be set. Cyclic references are
  2146→	// unrolled to a limited degree and, as such, may only be used within
  2147→	// non-required properties. (Nullable properties are not sufficient.) If
  2148→	// `$ref` is set on a sub-schema, no other properties, except for than those
  2149→	// starting as a `$`, may be set.
  2150→	ResponseJsonSchema any `json:"responseJsonSchema,omitempty"`
  2151→	// Optional. Configuration for model router requests.
  2152→	RoutingConfig *GenerationConfigRoutingConfig `json:"routingConfig,omitempty"`
  2153→	// Optional. Configuration for model selection.
  2154→	ModelSelectionConfig *ModelSelectionConfig `json:"modelSelectionConfig,omitempty"`
  2155→	// Optional. Safety settings in the request to block unsafe content in the
  2156→	// response.
  2157→	SafetySettings []*SafetySetting `json:"safetySettings,omitempty"`
  2158→	// Optional. Code that enables the system to interact with external systems to
  2159→	// perform an action outside of the knowledge and scope of the model.
  2160→	Tools []*Tool `json:"tools,omitempty"`
  2161→	// Optional. Associates model output to a specific function call.
  2162→	ToolConfig *ToolConfig `json:"toolConfig,omitempty"`
  2163→	// Optional. Labels with user-defined metadata to break down billed charges.
  2164→	Labels map[string]string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
  2165→	// Optional. Resource name of a context cache that can be used in subsequent
  2166→	// requests.
  2167→	CachedContent string `json:"cachedContent,omitempty"`
  2168→	// Optional. The requested modalities of the response. Represents the set of
  2169→	// modalities that the model can return.
  2170→	ResponseModalities []string `json:"responseModalities,omitempty"`
  2171→	// Optional. If specified, the media resolution specified will be used.
  2172→	MediaResolution MediaResolution `json:"mediaResolution,omitempty"`
  2173→	// Optional. The speech generation configuration.
  2174→	SpeechConfig *SpeechConfig `json:"speechConfig,omitempty"`
  2175→	// Optional. If enabled, audio timestamp will be included in the request to the
  2176→	// model.
  2177→	AudioTimestamp bool `json:"audioTimestamp,omitempty"`
  2178→	// Optional. The thinking features configuration.
  2179→	ThinkingConfig *ThinkingConfig `json:"thinkingConfig,omitempty"`
  2180→	// Optional. The image generation configuration.
  2181→	ImageConfig *ImageConfig `json:"imageConfig,omitempty"`
  2182→	// Optional. Enables enhanced civic answers. It may not be available for all
  2183→	// models. This field is not supported in Vertex AI.
  2184→	EnableEnhancedCivicAnswers *bool `json:"enableEnhancedCivicAnswers,omitempty"`
  2185→}
  2186→
  2187→func (c GenerateContentConfig) ToGenerationConfig(backend Backend) (*GenerationConfig, error) {
  2188→	ac := apiClient{
  2189→		clientConfig: &ClientConfig{
  2190→			Backend: backend,
  2191→		},
  2192→	}
  2193→
  2194→	paramsMap := make(map[string]any)
  2195→	deepMarshal(c, &paramsMap)
  2196→	parentMap := make(map[string]any)
  2197→	var outputMap map[string]any
  2198→	var err error
  2199→	switch backend {
  2200→	case BackendGeminiAPI:
  2201→		outputMap, err = generateContentConfigToMldev(&ac, paramsMap, parentMap)
  2202→	case BackendVertexAI:
  2203→		outputMap, err = generateContentConfigToVertex(&ac, paramsMap, parentMap)
  2204→	default:
  2205→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Unsupported backend: %v", backend)
  2206→	}
  2207→
  2208→	if err != nil {
  2209→		return nil, err
  2210→	}
  2211→
  2212→	if len(parentMap) > 0 {
  2213→		keys := make([]string, 0, len(parentMap))
  2214→		for k := range parentMap {
  2215→			keys = append(keys, k)
  2216→		}
  2217→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Unsupported conversion for %v", keys)
  2218→	}

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1442→type FunctionDeclaration struct {
  1443→	// Optional. Defines the function behavior.
  1444→	Behavior Behavior `json:"behavior,omitempty"`
  1445→	// Optional. Description and purpose of the function. Model uses it to decide how and
  1446→	// whether to call the function.

  1456→	// names must start with a letter or an underscore and must only contain chars a-z,

1. ~/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/adk@v0.5.0/model/llm.go - The LLM interface, LLMRequest, LLMResponse types
2. ~/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/genai@v1.40.0/types.go or similar - The genai.Content, genai.Part, genai.FunctionCall, genai.FunctionResponse types. Also genai.Tool and genai.FunctionDeclaration types.
3. ~/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/genai@v1.40.0/client.go or similar - The genai.GenerateContentConfig type

I need to know:
- How genai.Content is structured (Role, Parts)
- How genai.Part represents text vs function calls vs function responses
- How genai.Tool / genai.FunctionDeclaration are structured (for tool definitions)
- How genai.GenerateContentConfig works (temperature, etc.)
- The full LLMResponse fields

Also check the fork's converter file to understand the mapping:
Run: gh api repos/jiatianzhao/adk-go-openai/contents/model/openai/converters.go --jq '.content' | base64 -d

This will show how the fork converts between genai types and OpenAI format.

content.text:
Perfect. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of the initialization flow in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/cmd/server/main.go`:

## 1. Environment Variables Read at Startup

From `main()` lines 24-26:

```go
dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
addr := envOr("LISTEN_ADDR", ":8080")
noAuth := os.Getenv("NO_AUTH") == "true"
```

These map to the CLAUDE.md environment variables table:
- **DATABASE_URL**: PostgreSQL connection (default: `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable`)
- **LISTEN_ADDR**: HTTP server bind address (default: `:8080`)
- **NO_AUTH**: Boolean flag (checked but not in CLAUDE.md table; enables/disables API authentication)
- **GOOGLE_API_KEY**: Not explicitly read in main.go, but passed implicitly to `agentpkg.NewOrchestrator()` (line 54) which internally checks it via `os.Getenv()` to initialize the agent

## 2. Overall Initialization Flow

**Phase 1: Database Setup (lines 29-39)**
- Create connection pool from `DATABASE_URL`
- Run migrations

**Phase 2: Worker Registration (lines 41-61)**
- Create initial `pgStore` with `nil` riverClient (pre-queue setup)
- Register `NotifyWorker` unconditionally
- Conditionally create and register `AgentWorker`:
  - Calls `agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore)` 
  - If GOOGLE_API_KEY is unavailable, this errors; agent work is skipped with a warning
  - Otherwise, creates `AgentWorker` and registers it

**Phase 3: River Queue Initialization (lines 63-76)**
- Initialize River client with registered workers
- Start River background processing

**Phase 4: Re-initialize pgStore with River Client (line 71)**
- Update `pgStore` reference with the now-initialized River client
- This allows `TriggerAgentRun` to actually enqueue agent jobs

**Phase 5: Ingestion & API Setup (lines 78-104)**
- Create ingestion layer (polling orchestrator)
- Create broadcaster for SSE notifications
- Register all API routes with dependencies

**Phase 6: Run Background Services (lines 109-120)**
- Start polling orchestrator
- Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY → SSE bridge
- Start HTTP server

## 3. How NewOrchestrator Is Called

**Line 54:**
```go
agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore)
```

Signature: `NewOrchestrator(store *PgStore) (*Orchestrator, error)`

Internally, this function:
- Checks for `GOOGLE_API_KEY` environment variable
- If missing, returns an error (caught on line 55-56, logged as warning)
- If present, initializes the ADK-Go agent infrastructure and returns an `*Orchestrator`

The store passed is initially created with `nil` riverClient, so `NewOrchestrator` cannot enqueue jobs itself — only the agent worker can trigger runs once River is initialized.

## 4. How Agent Worker Is Registered

**Lines 54-61:**
```go
agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore)
if err != nil {
    slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available — agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
} else {
    agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
    slog.Info("agent worker registered")
}
```

Registration happens **only if** `NewOrchestrator` succeeds (i.e., GOOGLE_API_KEY is set). If the key is missing:
- No error crashes the server
- A warning is logged
- Agent jobs enqueued by `NotifyWorker` remain in the queue unprocessed until the server restarts with the key set

## Key Architectural Insight

The initialization exhibits careful ordering:
1. **Workers registered first** (before River client starts)
2. **River client created and started** (activates the queue)
3. **pgStore re-initialized** with River client (enables job enqueueing)

This two-phase `pgStore` creation prevents race conditions where the store might try to enqueue jobs before the River client exists.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide a comprehensive summary:

## ADK Model.LLM Interface Type Reference

Based on the ADK v0.5.0 module and the OpenAI adapter fork, here's the complete type reference for building an OpenAI adapter:

### **Core LLM Interface**
```go
// Location: google.golang.org/adk@v0.5.0/model/llm.go
type LLM interface {
	Name() string
	GenerateContent(ctx context.Context, req *LLMRequest, stream bool) iter.Seq2[*LLMResponse, error]
}

type LLMRequest struct {
	Model    string
	Contents []*genai.Content
	Config   *genai.GenerateContentConfig
	Tools map[string]any `json:"-"`
}
```

---

### **genai.Content Structure**
```go
// Location: google.golang.org/genai@v1.40.0/types.go
type Content struct {
	Parts []*Part  // List of parts that constitute a message (text, functions, files, etc.)
	Role  string   // "user" or "model" (defaults to "user" if empty)
}

const (
	RoleUser  = "user"
	RoleModel = "model"
)
```

---

### **genai.Part Structure** (Multimodal Union Type)
```go
type Part struct {
	// Text content
	Text string
	
	// Function calling
	FunctionCall *FunctionCall           // Model calls a function
	FunctionResponse *FunctionResponse   // User responds to function call
	
	// Code execution
	ExecutableCode *ExecutableCode       // Code to execute
	CodeExecutionResult *CodeExecutionResult  // Result of execution
	
	// File/media
	InlineData *Blob                     // Base64 encoded bytes + MIME type
	FileData *FileData                   // File URI + MIME type
	
	// Other
	Thought bool                         // Whether this is a thought
	ThoughtSignature []byte              // Signature for reusing thoughts
	VideoMetadata *VideoMetadata         // Metadata if video is present
	MediaResolution *PartMediaResolution // Media resolution info
}
```

---

### **genai.FunctionCall**
```go
type FunctionCall struct {
	ID   string         // Unique identifier (CRITICAL for OpenAI API matching)
	Name string         // Function name to call
	Args map[string]any // Function parameters as JSON object
	
	// Streaming support
	PartialArgs []*PartialArg // Incremental arguments during streaming
	WillContinue *bool         // Whether more arguments follow
}
```

---

### **genai.FunctionResponse**
```go
type FunctionResponse struct {
	ID   string         // Must match original tool_call_id (CRITICAL)
	Name string         // Function name that was called
	Response map[string]any  // Result as JSON object (use "output"/"error" keys)
	
	// Advanced
	Parts []*FunctionResponsePart  // For multimodal responses
	WillContinue *bool             // Generator mode
	Scheduling FunctionResponseScheduling  // Response scheduling
}

type FunctionResponsePart struct {
	InlineData *FunctionResponseBlob      // Base64 bytes
	FileData *FunctionResponseFileData    // File URI
}
```

---

### **genai.Tool & genai.FunctionDeclaration**
```go
type Tool struct {
	FunctionDeclarations []*FunctionDeclaration // List of functions
	
	// Other tools (not needed for basic adapters)
	Retrieval *Retrieval
	GoogleSearchRetrieval *GoogleSearchRetrieval
	ComputerUse *ComputerUse
	FileSearch *FileSearch
	CodeExecution *ToolCodeExecution
	// ... more specialized tools
}

type FunctionDeclaration struct {
	Name string                 // Function name (a-z, A-Z, 0-9, underscore, dot, dash)
	Description string          // What the function does
	Parameters *Schema           // JSON Schema defining input parameters
	ParametersJsonSchema any     // Alternative: raw JSON Schema object
	Response *Schema             // Output schema
	ResponseJsonSchema any       // Alternative: raw JSON Schema
	Behavior Behavior            // How model should use this function
}
```

---

### **genai.GenerateContentConfig**
```go
type GenerateContentConfig struct {
	SystemInstruction *Content            // System prompt
	
	// Generation parameters
	Temperature *float32                  // Randomness (0.0-2.0)
	TopP *float32                         // Nucleus sampling
	TopK *float32                         // Top-K sampling
	MaxOutputTokens int32                 // Max response length
	CandidateCount int32                  // Number of responses to generate
	StopSequences []string                // Stop generation on these strings
	
	// Penalties & seed
	PresencePenalty *float32              // Penalize repeated tokens
	FrequencyPenalty *float32             // Penalize token frequency
	Seed *int32                           // Deterministic generation
	
	// Tool configuration
	Tools []*Tool                         // Available tools/functions
	ToolConfig *ToolConfig                // Tool calling configuration
	
	// Response format
	ResponseMIMEType string                // "application/json" for JSON mode
	ResponseSchema *Schema                // Structured output schema
	ResponseJsonSchema any                // Alternative JSON Schema
	
	// Other features
	SafetySettings []*SafetySetting       // Content filters
	Labels map[string]string              // Usage tracking labels
	CachedContent string                  // Context cache resource name
	ResponseModalities []string           // Expected response types
	SpeechConfig *SpeechConfig            // Text-to-speech config
	ThinkingConfig *ThinkingConfig        // Extended thinking
	ImageConfig *ImageConfig              // Image generation
	HTTPOptions *HTTPOptions              // Custom HTTP settings
}

type ToolConfig struct {
	FunctionCallingConfig *FunctionCallingConfig  // Function calling mode
	RetrievalConfig *RetrievalConfig            // Retrieval settings
}

type FunctionCallingConfig struct {
	Mode FunctionCallingConfigMode  // AUTO, ANY, NONE
	AllowedFunctionNames []string    // Restrict which functions to call
	StreamFunctionCallArguments *bool  // Stream function args incrementally
}
```

---

### **genai.FinishReason** (Enum)
```go
type FinishReason string

const (
	FinishReasonUnspecified FinishReason = "FINISH_REASON_UNSPECIFIED"
	FinishReasonStop                     = "STOP"              // Normal stop
	FinishReasonMaxTokens                = "MAX_TOKENS"        // Hit token limit
	FinishReasonSafety                   = "SAFETY"            // Safety filter
	FinishReasonRecitation               = "RECITATION"        // Copyright detection
	FinishReasonLanguage                 = "LANGUAGE"          // Unsupported language
	FinishReasonBlocklist                = "BLOCKLIST"         // Forbidden terms
	FinishReasonProhibitedContent        = "PROHIBITED_CONTENT"
	FinishReasonSPII                     = "SPII"              // Personal info detected
	FinishReasonMalformedFunctionCall    = "MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL"
	FinishReasonUnexpectedToolCall       = "UNEXPECTED_TOOL_CALL"
	// ... image-specific reasons
)
```

---

### **LLMResponse** (Complete)
```go
type LLMResponse struct {
	Content *genai.Content                         // Response content
	UsageMetadata *genai.GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata  // Token counts
	CitationMetadata *genai.CitationMetadata      // Source attribution
	GroundingMetadata *genai.GroundingMetadata    // Search results
	LogprobsResult *genai.LogprobsResult         // Token probabilities
	CustomMetadata map[string]any                 // Custom fields
	
	// Streaming flags
	Partial bool                                  // Incomplete streamed content
	TurnComplete bool                             // Full turn finished
	Interrupted bool                              // User interrupted
	
	// Completion info
	FinishReason genai.FinishReason               // Why generation stopped
	ErrorCode string                              // Error identifier
	ErrorMessage string                           // Error details
	AvgLogprobs float64                           // Average log probability
}
```

---

### **Key OpenAI Adapter Patterns** (from fork)

**1. Converting genai.Content → OpenAI Messages:**
- `genai.Content.Role` ("user"/"model") → `OpenAIMessage.Role` ("user"/"assistant")
- `genai.Part.Text` → `Content: "text"`
- `genai.Part.FunctionCall` → `ToolCalls: [{Type: "function", Function: {Name, Arguments}}]`
- `genai.Part.FunctionResponse` → Separate message with `Role: "tool"` and `ToolCallID`
- `genai.Part.InlineData` → Base64 data URL for images
- `genai.Part.FileData` → Direct URL reference

**2. Tool Conversion (three formats supported):**
```go
// Format 1: ADK tool with Declaration() method
if declarer, ok := toolDef.(interface{ Declaration() *genai.FunctionDeclaration }); ok
// Format 2: Tool with Name()/Description() methods
if describer, ok := toolDef.(interface{ Name() string; Description() string }); ok
// Format 3: Legacy map[string]any
if toolMap, ok := toolDef.(map[string]any); ok
```

**3. Session Management:**
- OpenAI conversation history is stateless - the adapter must track it per session
- Use context to pass session IDs: `WithSessionID(ctx, "user-id")`
- Store history and append new messages to it

**4. Critical Details for OpenAI Compatibility:**
- `FunctionCall.ID` must be preserved and matched in `FunctionResponse.ID`
- Missing IDs will cause 400 API errors
- JSON mode requires "JSON" keyword in system prompt
- Function arguments must be valid JSON with proper sanitization

---

### **File Locations in Module Cache**
- `/Users/pc/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/adk@v0.5.0/model/llm.go` — LLM interface
- `/Users/pc/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/genai@v1.40.0/types.go` — All genai types (6469 lines)
- `/Users/pc/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/genai@v1.40.0/client.go` — Client config

This reference should give you everything you need to build or debug an OpenAI adapter!

### **genai.GenerateContentConfig**
```go
type GenerateContentConfig struct {
	SystemInstruction *Content            // System prompt
	
	// Generation parameters
	Temperature *float32                  // Randomness (0.0-2.0)
	TopP *float32                         // Nucleus sampling
	TopK *float32                         // Top-K sampling
	MaxOutputTokens int32                 // Max response length
	CandidateCount int32                  // Number of responses to generate
	StopSequences []string                // Stop generation on these strings
	
	// Penalties & seed
	PresencePenalty *float32              // Penalize repeated tokens
	FrequencyPenalty *float32             // Penalize token frequency
	Seed *int32                           // Deterministic generation
	
	// Tool configuration
	Tools []*Tool                         // Available tools/functions
	ToolConfig *ToolConfig                // Tool calling configuration
	
	// Response format
	ResponseMIMEType string                // "application/json" for JSON mode
	ResponseSchema *Schema                // Structured output schema
	ResponseJsonSchema any                // Alternative JSON Schema
	
	// Other features
	SafetySettings []*SafetySetting       // Content filters
	Labels map[string]string              // Usage tracking labels
	CachedContent string                  // Context cache resource name
	ResponseModalities []string           // Expected response types
	SpeechConfig *SpeechConfig            // Text-to-speech config
	ThinkingConfig *ThinkingConfig        // Extended thinking
	ImageConfig *ImageConfig              // Image generation
	HTTPOptions *HTTPOptions              // Custom HTTP settings
}

type ToolConfig struct {
	FunctionCallingConfig *FunctionCallingConfig  // Function calling mode
	RetrievalConfig *RetrievalConfig            // Retrieval settings
}

type FunctionCallingConfig struct {
	Mode FunctionCallingConfigMode  // AUTO, ANY, NONE
	AllowedFunctionNames []string    // Restrict which functions to call
	StreamFunctionCallArguments *bool  // Stream function args incrementally
}
```

---

### **genai.FinishReason** (Enum)
```go
type FinishReason string

const (
	FinishReasonUnspecified FinishReason = "FINISH_REASON_UNSPECIFIED"
	FinishReasonStop                     = "STOP"              // Normal stop
	FinishReasonMaxTokens                = "MAX_TOKENS"        // Hit token limit
	FinishReasonSafety                   = "SAFETY"            // Safety filter
	FinishReasonRecitation               = "RECITATION"        // Copyright detection
	FinishReasonLanguage                 = "LANGUAGE"          // Unsupported language
	FinishReasonBlocklist                = "BLOCKLIST"         // Forbidden terms
	FinishReasonProhibitedContent        = "PROHIBITED_CONTENT"
	FinishReasonSPII                     = "SPII"              // Personal info detected
	FinishReasonMalformedFunctionCall    = "MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL"
	FinishReasonUnexpectedToolCall       = "UNEXPECTED_TOOL_CALL"
	// ... image-specific reasons
)
```

---

### **LLMResponse** (Complete)
```go
type LLMResponse struct {
	Content *genai.Content                         // Response content
	UsageMetadata *genai.GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata  // Token counts
	CitationMetadata *genai.CitationMetadata      // Source attribution
	GroundingMetadata *genai.GroundingMetadata    // Search results
	LogprobsResult *genai.LogprobsResult         // Token probabilities
	CustomMetadata map[string]any                 // Custom fields
	
	// Streaming flags
	Partial bool                                  // Incomplete streamed content
	TurnComplete bool                             // Full turn finished
	Interrupted bool                              // User interrupted
	
	// Completion info
	FinishReason genai.FinishReason               // Why generation stopped
	ErrorCode string                              // Error identifier
	ErrorMessage string                           // Error details
	AvgLogprobs float64                           // Average log probability
}
```

---

### **Key OpenAI Adapter Patterns** (from fork)

**1. Converting genai.Content → OpenAI Messages:**
- `genai.Content.Role` ("user"/"model") → `OpenAIMessage.Role` ("user"/"assistant")
- `genai.Part.Text` → `Content: "text"`
- `genai.Part.FunctionCall` → `ToolCalls: [{Type: "function", Function: {Name, Arguments}}]`
- `genai.Part.FunctionResponse` → Separate message with `Role: "tool"` and `ToolCallID`
- `genai.Part.InlineData` → Base64 data URL for images
- `genai.Part.FileData` → Direct URL reference

**2. Tool Conversion (three formats supported):**
```go
// Format 1: ADK tool with Declaration() method
if declarer, ok := toolDef.(interface{ Declaration() *genai.FunctionDeclaration }); ok
// Format 2: Tool with Name()/Description() methods
if describer, ok := toolDef.(interface{ Name() string; Description() string }); ok
// Format 3: Legacy map[string]any
if toolMap, ok := toolDef.(map[string]any); ok
```

**3. Session Management:**
- OpenAI conversation history is stateless - the adapter must track it per session
- Use context to pass session IDs: `WithSessionID(ctx, "user-id")`
- Store history and append new messages to it

**4. Critical Details for OpenAI Compatibility:**
- `FunctionCall.ID` must be preserved and matched in `FunctionResponse.ID`
- Missing IDs will cause 400 API errors
- JSON mode requires "JSON" keyword in system prompt
- Function arguments must be valid JSON with proper sanitization

---

### **File Locations in Module Cache**
- `/Users/pc/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/adk@v0.5.0/model/llm.go` — LLM interface
- `/Users/pc/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/genai@v1.40.0/types.go` — All genai types (6469 lines)
- `/Users/pc/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/genai@v1.40.0/client.go` — Client config

This reference should give you everything you need to build or debug an OpenAI adapter!

content.text:
Key insight: the Gemini adapter **doesn't use `req.Tools` at all**. Tools are passed via `req.Config.Tools` (the `genai.GenerateContentConfig.Tools` field). The `req.Tools map[string]any` field isn't used by the Gemini adapter — it passes `req.Config` directly to the Gemini API which already has `Tools []*genai.Tool`.

So the OpenAI adapter needs to read tools from `req.Config.Tools` and convert `genai.FunctionDeclaration` to OpenAI function format.

input.content:
# Plan: Add OpenAI Model Support

## Context

Agent runs currently only work with Gemini (hardcoded `gemini-2.5-flash`). The user wants to use OpenAI models (e.g. `gpt-5.2`) as an alternative provider. We'll write a lightweight in-repo OpenAI adapter implementing the ADK `model.LLM` interface, and add a factory to switch between providers via env vars.

Reference: [jiatianzhao/adk-go-openai](https://github.com/jiatianzhao/adk-go-openai) for conversion patterns.

## Steps

### 1. Create `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` — OpenAI adapter

New file implementing `model.LLM` interface (2 methods: `Name()`, `GenerateContent()`).

Key responsibilities:
- Convert `req.Config.Tools` (`[]*genai.Tool` containing `FunctionDeclaration`s) → OpenAI `tools` array format
- Convert `req.Contents` (`[]*genai.Content`) → OpenAI messages:
  - Role `"user"` → `"user"`, role `"model"` → `"assistant"`
  - `Part.Text` → message content string
  - `Part.FunctionCall` → assistant message with `tool_calls` array
  - `Part.FunctionResponse` → `"tool"` role message with `tool_call_id`
- Convert `req.Config.SystemInstruction` → `"system"` role message
- Map config fields: `Temperature`, `TopP`, `MaxOutputTokens`, `StopSequences`
- Make HTTP POST to `{baseURL}/chat/completions`
- Convert response back to `*model.LLMResponse` with `genai.Content`:
  - Text → `genai.Part{Text: ...}`
  - `tool_calls` → `genai.Part{FunctionCall: &genai.FunctionCall{ID, Name, Args}}`
  - `finish_reason` → `genai.FinishReason`
- Non-streaming only (stream=false returns single-yield iterator, stream=true same behavior)
- Include `Close()` method (no-op, no background goroutines needed since we don't track sessions)

### 2. Create `internal/agent/model.go` — Model factory

New file with factory function:

```go
type LLMConfig struct {
    Provider string // "gemini" or "openai"
    Model    string // e.g. "gemini-2.5-flash", "gpt-5.2"
    // Gemini
    GoogleAPIKey string
    // OpenAI
    OpenAIAPIKey  string
    OpenAIBaseURL string
}

func NewLLMModel(ctx context.Context, cfg LLMConfig) (model.LLM, error)
```

Switches on `cfg.Provider`, calls either `gemini.NewModel()` or `openai.NewModel()`.

### 3. Modify `internal/agent/orchestrator.go`

- Change `Orchestrator` struct: replace `apiKey string` with `llmConfig LLMConfig`
- Change `NewOrchestrator(store, cfg LLMConfig)` signature — accept config instead of reading env vars internally
- Change `executeAgent()`: call `NewLLMModel(ctx, o.llmConfig)` instead of hardcoded `gemini.NewModel()`
- Remove `defaultModel` constant (model name now comes from config)

### 4. Modify `cmd/server/main.go`

- Read new env vars: `LLM_PROVIDER`, `LLM_MODEL`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_BASE_URL`
- Build `LLMConfig` and pass to `NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig)`
- Default: `provider=gemini`, `model=gemini-2.5-flash`

### 5. Update `CLAUDE.md`

Add new env vars to the environment variables table.

## Files

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` | Create — OpenAI adapter (~250 lines) |
| `internal/agent/model.go` | Create — factory function (~40 lines) |
| `internal/agent/orchestrator.go` | Modify — use factory, accept config |
| `cmd/server/main.go` | Modify — read env vars, build config |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Modify — document new env vars |

## Verification

1. `go build ./...` — compiles cleanly
2. `go test ./internal/agent/...` — existing tests pass
3. `go vet ./...` — no issues
4. Manual test: set `LLM_PROVIDER=openai LLM_MODEL=gpt-5.2 OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... make run` and trigger an agent run
5. Manual test: default env (no LLM_PROVIDER set) still works with Gemini
